ddls:
CREATE TABLE "processing_centers" (
  "processing_center_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each processing center */,
  "center_name" TEXT /* Name of the processing center */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the processing center */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the processing center */,
  "center_type" TEXT /* Type of the processing center (e.g., regional, national) */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the processing center */,
  "center_director" TEXT /* Name of the center director */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of cases the center can handle */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("processing_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wage_data" (
  "wage_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wage data entry */,
  "case_no" TEXT /* Reference to the case */,
  "offered_from" REAL /* Lower bound of the wage offered */,
  "offered_to" REAL /* Upper bound of the wage offered */,
  "offered_per" TEXT /* Unit of the wage offered (e.g., Year, Hour) */,
  "prevailing_amount" REAL /* Amount of the prevailing wage */,
  "prevailing_level" TEXT /* Level of the prevailing wage */,
  "soc_code" TEXT /* SOC code for the prevailing wage job */,
  "soc_title" TEXT /* Title of the SOC code */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the prevailing wage data */,
  "other_source" TEXT /* Other sources of the prevailing wage data */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date when the wage data is effective */,
  "expiry_date" TEXT /* Date when the wage data expires */,
  "wage_source_url" TEXT /* URL to the source of the wage data */,
  "wage_source_document" TEXT /* Reference to any documents that support the wage data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("wage_data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., case officer, supervisor, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
